qa_pairs.question: How do you upload an image to the New Arbutus Cloud using the OpenStack command-line interface?
qa_pairs.answer: To upload an image to the New Arbutus Cloud using the OpenStack command-line interface (CLI), use the command `openstack --os-cloud <cloud> image create --private --file <file> <newImageName>`. You need to replace `<cloud>` with your specific cloud, `<file>` with the name of the image file, and `<newImageName>` with your desired name for the new image.

qa_pairs.question: After an image has been uploaded, how can a volume be created from it in the New Arbutus Cloud web user interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Once an image has been uploaded to the New Arbutus Cloud, you can create a volume from it via the web user interface (UI). Navigate to "Compute -> Images", find the uploaded image, and select the "Create Volume" option from its drop-down menu. This will create a new volume that can then be attached to instances or used to boot a new instance.
